finding: The ordering of the §5.5 drop rules relative to per-role selection and caps is unspecified. §5.2 selects and caps from the raw universe ('first 10 files in git diff base..HEAD (alphabetical)', 'first 20 files of git diff base..HEAD', security 'ranked alphabetical' with the §5.1 cap of 20) and §5.5 separately says 'Across all roles, drop: binary files, testdata/**, *.golden, deleted files' — without saying whether drops filter the universe before selection or prune the already-capped lists. With a diff of 12 files whose first 10 alphabetical entries include 3 golden fixtures, tester A (drop-first) expects the maintainability list to backfill to 10 eligible files, while tester B (cap-then-drop, reading §5.2's 'first 10 ... regardless of content' literally and applying §5.5 afterward) expects 7 entries. Diffs containing testdata/ and *.golden files are routine in this repo, so the two readings produce contradictory tests for mainline affected_files output; §19.1's 'TestFilterFiles_SpecCoverage_Cap20: 50 input files yields ≤ 20 entries' is satisfiable under both readings and does not disambiguate.

resolved.evidence: Round-6 revision applied to spec/0.23.0.md §5.5/§19.1: drop rules declared to filter the file universe before all per-role selection, ranking, and caps (backfill semantics); TestFilterFiles_DropFirstBackfill pins the ordering

suggestion: State the ordering once in §5.2 or §5.5: the file universe (git diff base..HEAD, or the --affected-files list) has the §5.5 drop rules applied FIRST; every per-role selection rule, ranking, and cap in §5.1-§5.2 operates on the filtered universe, so caps backfill with the next eligible files. Optionally pin it with a test: 12-file diff whose first 10 alphabetical entries include 3 fixtures yields a 10-entry maintainability list.
